# Wave H4 — Visibility + Stat Depth

## Goal
Make the site easier to read and surface data we already collect. From `2026-04-23-improvements-plan.md`, picking highest-value Wave I + J + K items into one 6-lane fleet.

## Lane table

| Lane | Fleet | Size | Scope | Owns | Blocked by |
|------|-------|------|-------|------|------------|
| 1 | Han 😉🚀 | M | Anomaly banner on game/player when player_goals_sum > team_score | `views/game.ts`, `views/gameDetail.ts`, `views/playerDetail.ts`, NEW `components/anomalyBanner.ts` | — |
| 2 | Yoda 👽✨ | M | Header search-as-you-type | NEW `server/src/routes/search.ts`, NEW `web/src/components/searchBox.ts`, `api.ts`, `main.ts` (insert search bar in header only) | — |
| 3 | Leia 👑💁‍♀️ | S | `/status` page + "last updated" footer line on dashboard | NEW `views/status.ts`, `views/dashboard.ts` (footer line only), `router.ts` add route | — |
| 4 | Chewy 🐻💪 | S | Mobile responsive grid + schedule newest-first | `styles.css`, `views/schedule.ts` sort | — |
| 5 | R2 🤖🔧 | S | Goalie saves + faceoff leaderboards | `server/src/routes/leaders.ts` add stats, `web/src/views/leaders.ts` add tabs | — |
| 6 | Luke 🌟⚔️ | S | Dead-route audit (`rivalries.ts`, `seasons.ts`) + nightly DB snapshot script | NEW `scripts/snapshotDb.ts`, possible deletes in `server/src/routes/`, `main.ts` (only nav links) | — |

## File boundary contract
- **`main.ts`**: only Yoda (header search) and Leia (router add) and Luke (nav links) touch it. Yoda inserts in `<header>` block. Leia adds `registerView('status', ...)` after existing registers. Luke adds nav `<a>` tags after existing nav links. Distinct line ranges.
- **`styles.css`**: Chewy owns exclusively. Other lanes: only use existing class names.
- **No lane** modifies `data/lacrosse.db`, `Dockerfile`, `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`.

## Validation
- `pnpm -r typecheck` clean
- `pnpm -r test` green
- `pnpm dev` boots; visit each new route and screenshot

## Wave H4 outcomes

- ✅ Lane 1 Han `e196678` — anomaly banner on game/player views
- ✅ Lane 2 Yoda `8595c1b` — header search-as-you-type (`/api/search`)
- ✅ Lane 3 Leia `85ab02c` — `/status` page + dashboard freshness footer
- ✅ Lane 4 Chewy `80862c5` — responsive dashboard + schedule newest-first
- ✅ Lane 5 R2 — no-op (already shipped); flagged "Mason Proctor and Javier Gonzalez-Cruz" parser-bug player row for next wave
- ✅ Lane 6 Luke `8149dad` — `snapshot:db` script + dead-route audit (`rivalries.ts` + `seasons.ts` both wired, no change)

Final: typecheck clean, 514 tests green, both remotes synced.

## Carry-forward to Wave H5
- Parser bug: investigate "Mason Proctor and Javier Gonzalez-Cruz" composite player row (R2 finding)
- Per-quarter chart on game detail (uses existing `game_periods` table)
- `confidence` badges on player_stats rows (column populated, never surfaced)
- Wire `snapshot:db` into a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow once `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` secret lands

## Wave H4 Retrospective

### Actual vs Estimated
- Lane 1 Han (M): ~4m → ✅ on target
- Lane 2 Yoda (M): ~3m → ✅ ahead
- Lane 3 Leia (S): ~4.5m → ⚠️ slightly over (S=expected ~3m); reasonable, freshness route inspection took time
- Lane 4 Chewy (S): ~1.5m → ✅ way ahead
- Lane 5 R2 (S): ~2m → ✅ ahead, was a no-op
- Lane 6 Luke (S): ~2.5m → ✅ on target

### Critical path
- All 6 lanes parallel, no blockers; longest lane (Han) drove wall clock (~4m)

### What went well
- Hard file boundaries on `main.ts` + `styles.css` prevented merge conflicts entirely
- All commits landed cleanly via simple `git pull --rebase` then push to legacy
- Sub-agents auto-discovered prior-shipped work (R2) instead of duplicating

### What to improve for Wave H5
- Plan was slightly stale for R2 (used `stat=` instead of actual `metric=`); orchestrator should grep route param names before drafting prompts
- Han's heuristic threshold (12 goals/game) for player-row anomaly is brittle — H5 lane should make it data-driven (3σ above season mean per position?)

## Wave H5 Proposal

### Lane plan (3 lanes, balanced M/M/S)

| Lane | Fleet | Effort | Scope | Blocked by | Status |
|------|-------|--------|-------|------------|--------|
| 1 | Han 😉🚀 | M | Composite-name parser fix + DB migration ("X and Y" → 2 players, redistribute stats) | — | Pending |
| 2 | Yoda 👽✨ | M | Per-quarter score chart on game detail using `game_periods` | — | Pending |
| 3 | Leia 👑💁‍♀️ | S | Confidence badges on player_stats rows in player + game detail | — | Pending |

Deferred: nightly snapshot workflow (blocked on `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` secret — user runbook).

## Wave H5 outcomes

- ✅ Lane 2 Yoda 👽✨ — per-quarter score chart on game detail. Discovery showed prior wave (Leia W14 L3) had already shipped: server `/api/games/:id` returns `periods` (game_id, teamId, periodNumber, goals), `GameDetail.periods: GamePeriod[]` typed in `web/src/api.ts`, and `gameDetail.ts` calls `renderQuarterByQuarter` (d3 grouped bar chart from `web/src/charts/quarterByQuarter.ts`) between scoreboard and player tables, no-op when `periods.length === 0`. Remaining gap closed: added 2 vitest cases in `games.test.ts` asserting periods array shape (8-row payload for game 10, empty array for game 11) and re-exported `GamePeriod` from `api.ts` for explicit typing. Tested locally against game_id=10 (Parkland 8 — Nazareth 7, 2026-04-21, 7-period payload incl. OTs). Chart approach: existing d3 helper `renderQuarterByQuarter` (no CSS-bar fallback needed). Validation: `pnpm -r typecheck` clean, 116 server tests + full web suite green.
